unit: Ὅ6 Unit 6: Systems of Linear Equations and Inequalities

questions.question: A football player scores $x$ points in a game and $y$ points in the next game. If $x + y > 10$, what is the solution region?
Which of the following inequalities represents the region below the line $y = x - 2$?
A basketball player has a free throw percentage of $x%$ and a field goal percentage of $y%$. If $x + y > 80$, what type of line is the boundary?
Which inequality represents the region to the right of the line $x = -3$?
A cyclist travels $x$ miles in the first hour and $y$ miles in the second hour. If $x + y geq 20$, what type of boundary line is represented?
Graph the inequality $y >  rac{1}{2}x - 1$ and identify the solution region. Provide at least 3 test points to verify your solution.

A gymnast's score $y$ is determined by the equation $y leq 3x + 2$, where $x$ is the level of difficulty. Graph this inequality and find the solution region. What is the lowest possible score a gymnast can achieve if the level of difficulty is $x = 2$?

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Emphasize the importance of dashed and solid boundary lines.
Tip 2: Use real-world examples to illustrate the concept of linear inequalities.
Tip 3: Encourage students to use graph paper when solving problems.
